response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior individual contributor with broad full-stack, distributed-systems, and testing expertise, plus recent exposure to AI-agent tooling (MCP code generators). However, the role is an executive CTO position requiring demonstrated production multi-agent AI systems, experience scaling engineering teams from 10 to 30+, senior leadership at scale-ups, and governance of hybrid delivery models — none of which appear in this resume. The gap between a staff-level IC profile and the executive scope of this role is too large to recommend an interview.
strengths: Deep, versatile engineering background (backend, low-latency microservices at millions of req/s, embedded, web) spanning 15+ years
Hands-on AI-agent infrastructure exposure (MCP code generators, agentic workflow tooling) at current role
Strong software craftsmanship discipline — TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, open-source authorship, teaching/workshops
concerns: No executive or senior technical-leadership experience; no evidence of scaling teams, developing leadership layers, or board-level communication
No production multi-agent AI systems experience — AI exposure is recent and tooling-focused, not operating stateful agents with financial consequences
Limited fintech depth — brief investment-platform and pricing work, but no credit systems, risk modeling, or regulated-environment AI operations

key_factors.factor: Lacks production multi-agent AI systems experience
